[faculty, clubs, infrastructure] Campus Amenities Discover our world-class facilities and student amenities 1 / 2 Green Campus We proudly recieved “Swatch Campus Award “.The green campus of the College holds a healthy medicinal garden which is a collection of varities of plants having high medicinal values. We can also see plant species which are very rare. 1 / 2 Transportation College is providing transportation facilities to various localities. There are total 21 buses for the college. Board Room Excellent A/C Room for Executive meeting Multimedia Conference Room Well equipped A/C Conference Room with a capacity of 150 occupants for executive meetings and video conferences.

[faculty, clubs, infrastructure] Board Room Excellent A/C Room for Executive meeting Multimedia Conference Room Well equipped A/C Conference Room with a capacity of 150 occupants for executive meetings and video conferences. Snacks Bar We have three snacks bar which are located at bio block, indoor stadium and near to the canteen. 1 / 3 Auditorium We have a well-built auditorium with a maximum occupancy of 3000. All major programmes of college are conducted in this auditorium. 1 / 2 Fitness Center A student not only want to be mentally strong but also he/she want to be physically fit. There are separate well equipped physical fitness centre for boys and girls.

[faculty, clubs, infrastructure] There are separate well equipped physical fitness centre for boys and girls. Proper guidance is provided by the fitness trainers .Its open at both morning and evening. Reprographic Cetnter We have two Reprographic centres in the college campus which are located at Bio Block, Decennial block and at Auditorium. 1 / 2 Canteen In Sahrdaya we have a clean and hygenic canteen that serves food for students as well as faculty members. We have a proper menu of food which is specific for each day. Fixed time schedule is also provided for food delivery. 1 / 3 Hostel Three hostels named Shalome, Jyothis and Thejus are allotted for girls and Santhome hostel for boys.

[faculty, clubs, infrastructure] 1 / 3 Hostel Three hostels named Shalome, Jyothis and Thejus are allotted for girls and Santhome hostel for boys. We have a separate PG hostel block too. All the hostels are ragging free and it’s truly a home away from home. Chapel

[general] In order to  enhance the spirituality among Sahrdayans we have a chapel . Every day the holy mass is being celebrated in the chapel under the guidance of Fathers and Sisters. We also conduct holy rosary everyday and adorations on every Mondays and Thursday ATM In house South Indian Bank ATM, so that Sahrdayans can withdraw can at any convenient time without any hassle.

[department_cse, department_ece, department_eee, department_ce, department_bt, department_bme, research, clubs, infrastructure, about] About Us Discover our institution's story and values Institution Overview Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology, Kodakara Established by the Diocese of Irinjalakuda, Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology was founded with the vision of imparting value-based, quality technical education that integrates professional competence with ethical responsibility. The institution is managed by the Irinjalakuda Diocesan Educational Trust, registered in 2001, under the visionary leadership of His Excellency Mar James Pazhayattil, the first Bishop of the Diocese.

[department_cse, department_ece, department_eee, department_ce, department_bt, department_bme, research, clubs, infrastructure, about] The institution is managed by the Irinjalakuda Diocesan Educational Trust, registered in 2001, under the visionary leadership of His Excellency Mar James Pazhayattil, the first Bishop of the Diocese. The college was established in 2002 with approval from the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) and was initially affiliated to the University of Calicut. Located on a picturesque 25-acre green campus at Kodakara, Thrissur District, the institution provides a serene and intellectually stimulating environment conducive to academic excellence, innovation, and holistic development.

[department_cse, department_ece, department_eee, department_ce, department_bt, department_bme, research, clubs, infrastructure, about] Located on a picturesque 25-acre green campus at Kodakara, Thrissur District, the institution provides a serene and intellectually stimulating environment conducive to academic excellence, innovation, and holistic development. In alignment with the restructuring of technical education in the State, the institution transitioned in 2015 to affiliation with APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University (APJAKTU), Kerala.

[department_cse, department_ece, department_eee, department_ce, department_bt, department_bme, research, clubs, infrastructure, about] In alignment with the restructuring of technical education in the State, the institution transitioned in 2015 to affiliation with APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University (APJAKTU), Kerala. Over the years, Sahrdaya has expanded its academic portfolio to offer six undergraduate (B.Tech) programmes:  Biomedical Engineering  Biotechnology Engineering  Civil Engineering  Computer Science and Engineering  Electronics and Communication Engineering  Electrical and Electronics Engineering The institution also offers four postgraduate (M.Tech) programmes:  Biomedical Engineering  Computer Science and Engineering  Industrial Biotechnology  Embedded Systems In addition to undergraduate and postgraduate programmes, the institution offers PhD research programmes in approved disciplines, fostering a strong research culture a

[research, governance, infrastructure, about, mou] nd enabling scholars to pursue advanced studies aligned with contemporary technological and societal needs. The college also offers Postgraduate Diploma programmes in emerging and interdisciplinary areas, aimed at enhancing professional competencies, upskilling graduates, and supporting lifelong learning in response to evolving industry and research demands. In recognition of its academic maturity and governance practices, Academic Autonomy was conferred by the University Grants Commission (UGC) in 2024.

[research, governance, infrastructure, about, mou] In recognition of its academic maturity and governance practices, Academic Autonomy was conferred by the University Grants Commission (UGC) in 2024. This milestone empowered the institution to implement Outcome-Based Education (OBE), the Choice-Based Credit System (CBCS), and flexible, industry-aligned curricula, promoting interdisciplinary learning, innovation, and skill development. Sahrdaya is NAAC-accredited, and all undergraduate engineering programmes are NBA accredited by 2025, reflecting sustained adherence to national quality benchmarks.

[research, governance, infrastructure, about, mou] Sahrdaya is NAAC-accredited, and all undergraduate engineering programmes are NBA accredited by 2025, reflecting sustained adherence to national quality benchmarks. The institution is well regarded among engineering colleges in Kerala for its disciplined academic environment, consistent student outcomes, and strong value system. The college provides state-of-the-art laboratories, ICT-enabled classrooms, a fully digitized central library, a language laboratory, modern computing facilities, well-maintained hostels, transport services, and comprehensive sports and fitness infrastructure. The lush green campus and eco-friendly initiatives contribute to a vibrant and enriching campus life.

[research, governance, infrastructure, about, mou] The lush green campus and eco-friendly initiatives contribute to a vibrant and enriching campus life. Graduates of Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology have progressed to reputed industries, higher education programmes, research institutions, and entrepreneurial ventures, reflecting the institution’s sustained emphasis on academic rigour, professional competence, and lifelong learning. The curriculum design, skill-oriented learning practices, an

[research, governance, clubs, infrastructure, about, mou] d industry exposure collectively support students in building successful and meaningful career pathways. Rooted in its founding values, Sahrdaya emphasizes holistic education, nurturing intellectual, physical, emotional, moral, and spiritual growth. Through value education, community service, mentoring, and inclusive campus practices, the institution fosters ethical leadership, social responsibility, compassion, and integrity, while remaining open and welcoming to students from all backgrounds. Aligned with its Vision 2030, Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology aspires to emerge as a centre of excellence in autonomous engineering education, with strengthened focus on research, innovation, entrepreneurship, industry collaboration, global engagement, and sustainable development, shaping future-ready engineers and responsible global citizens.

Irinjalakuda Diocese is doing a excellent service meeting the needs of Education, Health and Social responsibilities.

[admissions, department_cse, department_ece, department_bt, department_bme, clubs, about] Irinjalakuda Diocese is doing a excellent service meeting the needs of Education, Health and Social responsibilities. Considering the demands on the necessity of value based higher technical education, the people of the Diocese decided to establish an Engineering college to mark The Episcopal Silver Jubilee Year of His Excellency Bishop Mar James Pazhayattil and Silver Jubilee of the formation of the Diocese of Irinjalakuda .The Irinjalakuda Diocesan Educational Trust was formed and registered on 23-07-2001 with Reg. No: 138/IV 2001 at Irinjalakuda registration office with His Excellency Mar James Pazhayattil as Chairman, Rev. Msgr. Sebastian Ezhekadan as President, Rev. Fr.

[admissions, department_cse, department_ece, department_bt, department_bme, clubs, about] Fr. Joseph Thekkethala as Secretary, Rev.Fr. Pius Chirapanath as Finance Officer. On 21st November 2001 His Excellency Mar James Pazhayattil laid the foundation stone for central building of Sahrdaya College. Architects Mayphils from Ernakulam prepared the master plan for the college and detail plan for the individual buildings. On 22/02/2002 His Excellency Mar James Pazhayattil laid the foundation stone for the workshop and hostel building in the campus. In a short span of seven months the central building, workshop and hostel block were completed in all respects.

A drawing hall containing modern adjustable drawing boards was also made available for use. An advanced Computer Lab with 60 networked Pentium-4 terminals was established.

The club aims to foster innovation, analytical thinking, and practical learning through workshops, expert talks, competitions, and collaborative projects.

[faculty, department_bme, announcement, clubs, infrastructure, about] The club aims to foster innovation, analytical thinking, and practical learning through workshops, expert talks, competitions, and collaborative projects. The inauguration ceremony will feature an invited talk by Prof. Dr. Sabu M K, Professor, Department of Computer Applications, Cochin University of Science and Technology, on the topic “Retrieval Augmented Generation: Enhancing LLMs with External Data.” The session will provide valuable insights into emerging AI technologies and their real-world applications. [examination, governance, clubs, mou] Examination Conduct Guidelines and Regulations CONDUCT OF EXAMINATIONS & EVALUATION 1 Distribution of Examination Materials The Examination Materials will be distributed from the Autonomous Examination Cell. 2 Invigilation Invigilators shall be responsible for the fair conduct of the examination inside the examination hall. After the commencement of the examination, allotted bar codes shall be pasted on the space provided in the answer booklet and attendance sheet. The candidate's attendance shall be recorded by obtaining signature in the attendance sheet provided which shall be countersigned by the invigilator. Invigilators shall ensure that all candidates have returned the answer scripts.

[examination, governance, clubs, mou] Invigilators shall ensure that all candidates have returned the answer scripts. The answer scripts shall be handed over to the examination control room within 15 minutes after the examination. In case a candidate is found to have indulged in malpractice, his/her answer script shall be separately handed over to the CoE. Instructions to the Invigilators are given in Annexure I. 3 Collection and Bundling of Answer Scripts The answer scripts from the examination halls shall be received at the examination control room after each examination.

[examination, governance, clubs, mou] 3 Collection and Bundling of Answer Scripts The answer scripts from the examination halls shall be received at the examination control room after each examination. Answer scripts shall be bundled separately for each course and details such as the name of examination, date of examination, packet code, course code and course name, semester and the number of answer scripts shall be entered on the packing slip. The packed and sealed bundles shall be kept in the safe custody of the DyC(Ex) till the despatch to the evaluation camp. 4. Academic Discipline and Malpractices in Examinations Every student is required to observe discipline and decorous behaviour in the examination hall.

[examination, governance, clubs, mou] Academic Discipline and Malpractices in Examinations Every student is required to observe discipline and decorous behaviour in the examination hall. Any act of indiscipline, misbehaviour and unfair practice in examinations shall be punishable according to the nature

[examination] of offense committed by the student. 5. Post Examination The major activities involved during this phase includes the collection and verification of answer scripts, distribution of answer scripts to the examiners, valuation of answer scripts by various levels of examiners, providing necessary instructions and monitoring the valuation process, tabulation of marks and return of the valued answer scripts to the camp for revaluation purpose. All the activities of this phase will be under the direct control of CoE and Dyc(Ev). 6. Appointment of Evaluators ●The CoE shall appoint evaluators from the panel of examiners nominated by the departments to evaluate the answer scripts.

[examination] Appointment of Evaluators ●The CoE shall appoint evaluators from the panel of examiners nominated by the departments to evaluate the answer scripts. ●Evaluators shall have handled the course at least once. ●The number of evaluators appointed for each course shall be based on the number of candidates registered. ●There shall be three evaluators for the evaluation in a given term for a given course. ●Communication regarding the details of answer scripts allotted for evaluation shall be sent to evaluators in advance. 7 Valuation Process 1.Initial Evaluation by Two Evaluators: ●All answer scripts will be evaluated by two evaluators.

[examination] 7 Valuation Process 1.Initial Evaluation by Two Evaluators: ●All answer scripts will be evaluated by two evaluators. 2.Variation in Marks (≤ 15% of Maximum Marks): ●If the variation in total marks scored between the two evaluations is less than or equal to 15% of the maximum possible marks, the average mark (calculated question-wise) will be considered in the final tabulation of results. 3.Variation in Marks (> 15% of Maximum Marks): ●If the variation exceeds 15% of the maximum possible marks, the answer script will be sent for a third evaluation by an evaluator appointed by the Controller of Examinations (CoE).

[examination] 3.Variation in Marks (> 15% of Maximum Marks): ●If the variation exceeds 15% of the maximum possible marks, the answer script will be sent for a third evaluation by an evaluator appointed by the Controller of Examinations (CoE). ●Final Mark Calculation: The final marks will be the average of the two closest scores obtained from the three evaluations. This

[faculty, examination, research, announcement, governance, infrastructure] calculation will be done question- wise. (*) 8. Post Evaluation Audit ●Post evaluation audit shall be conducted for checking transparency and fairness of the evaluation. ●10% of evaluated answer scripts selected at random shall be audited by the expert. ●Adherence of evaluation to scheme and answer key provided shall be verified. ●Consistency of evaluation among answer scripts for a particular course shall be verified. ●Observations regarding inconsistency in evaluation observed if any shall be reported to the CoE. 9 .Uploading Marks Marks shall be uploaded by the evaluators and a hard copy from the portal by the evaluator concerned shall be handed over to CoE.

[faculty, examination, research, announcement, governance, infrastructure] 9 .Uploading Marks Marks shall be uploaded by the evaluators and a hard copy from the portal by the evaluator concerned shall be handed over to CoE. 10.Storage of Evaluated Answer Scripts ●The answer scripts after evaluation shall be bundled, properly recorded and kept in safe custody at the evaluation camp for easy retrieval for three months from the date of publication of results and subsequently stored in the strong room for four years. Answer scripts after four years of storage will be disposed of, as per the decisions of the Academic Council (AC). ●The DyC(Ev) shall hand over evaluated answer scripts for re-evaluation or other purposes on- demand within prescribed time limits. 11.

[faculty, examination, research, announcement, governance, infrastructure] 11. Laboratory / Project / Dissertation / Viva Voce The scheduling of all Practical Examinations shall be provided by the Head of the concerned Departments to the CoE sufficiently in advance of the scheduled commencement of the examinations. Evaluations in respect of the Laboratory Courses, Seminars, Mini Projects, Projects, Viva Voce examinations, etc., shall be duly completed by internal/external examiners appointed by COE from the panel submitted by the respective HOD. The faculty in charge shall upload the final marks to the portal within five working days after the examinatio

[governance, clubs, about] Overview Internal Quality Assurance Cell To keep up with the changes in higher education, educational Institution must actively engage themselves in the development and implementation of quality benchmarks in all their key performance areas. Committed to sustain its top ranking position, Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology has established the Internal Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC) in 2015 to institutionalize the process of quality enhancement and sustenance in all the key areas of its delivery.

[governance, clubs, about] Committed to sustain its top ranking position, Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology has established the Internal Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC) in 2015 to institutionalize the process of quality enhancement and sustenance in all the key areas of its delivery. The IQAC is acting as the nodal agency entrusted with the responsibility of ensuring total quality management by ushering in innovations leading to achieving excellence and adoption of intervention strategies for monitoring their successful implementation.

[governance, clubs, about] The IQAC is acting as the nodal agency entrusted with the responsibility of ensuring total quality management by ushering in innovations leading to achieving excellence and adoption of intervention strategies for monitoring their successful implementation. Ever in quest for excellence, the IQAC functions to assure institutional accountability with probity to all the stakeholders, funding agencies in particular and society as a whole. Since quality enhancement is a continuous process, the IQAC has become a part of the institution’s system and work towards realization of the goals of quality enhancement and sustenance.

[governance, clubs, about] Since quality enhancement is a continuous process, the IQAC has become a part of the institution’s system and work towards realization of the goals of quality enhancement and sustenance. [faculty, announcement, governance] Functions Key functions and responsibilities of IQAC IQAC Functions 1 Development and application of quality benchmarks/parameters for various academic and administrative activities of the institution 2 Facilitating the creation of a learner-centric environment conducive to quality education and faculty maturation to adopt the required knowledge and technology for participatory teaching and learning process 3 Arrangement for feedback response from students, parents and other stakeholders on quality-related institutional processes 4 Dissemination of information on various quality parameters of higher education 5 Organization of inter and intra institutional workshops, seminars on quality

[faculty, announcement, governance] related themes and promotion of quality circles 6 Documentation of the various programmes/activities leading to quality improvement 7 Acting as a nodal agency of the Institution for coordinating quality-related activities, including adoption and dissemination of best practices 8 Development and maintenance of institutional database through MIS for the purpose of maintaining/enhancing the institutional quality 9 Development of Quality Culture in the institution 10 Preparation of the Annual Quality Assurance Report (AQAR) as per guidelines and parameters of NAAC, to be submitted to NAAC

[governance] Objectives Core objectives and goals of IQAC The prime objective of the IQAC is to develop a system for conscious, consistent & catalytic improvement in the overall performance of institution. Key Objectives To develop a system for conscious, consistent, and catalytic action to improve the academic and administrative performance of the institution. To promote measures for institutional functioning towards quality enhancement through internalization of quality culture and institutionalization of best practices.
[general] NIRF National Institutional Ranking Framework NIRF Document View or  the National Institutional Ranking Framework document

[placement, research, governance, clubs, infrastructure, about] Quality Initiatives Quality Initiatives The Institutional Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC) enhances institutional quality through its supportive cells like ISO, RDC, IEDC, IIIC, and Placement & Training. IQAC conducts semester and annual planning meetings to align activities with the Strategic Action Plan 2030. It also organizes ISO management review meetings, ensuring adherence to ISO 9001:2015 QMS standards, where critical areas affecting quality are reviewed, feedback analyzed, and corrective actions implemented.
[search aliases: Innovation Entrepreneurship Development Centre]

[placement, research, governance, clubs, infrastructure, about] It also organizes ISO management review meetings, ensuring adherence to ISO 9001:2015 QMS standards, where critical areas affecting quality are reviewed, feedback analyzed, and corrective actions implemented. Key initiatives include the Innovation and Entrepreneurship Development Cell (IEDC), which earned recognition under Kerala Startup Mission's Local Entrepreneurship Advancement Programme (LEAP) and partnered with NASSCOM Startups. The Research Innovation Programme for women startups, funded by Kerala Startup Mission with a ₹25 lakh budget, supported 7 women researchers with grants up to ₹5 lakh. Four innovations from the college received recognition at the IEDC Idea Fest 2023.
[search aliases: Innovation Entrepreneurship Development Centre]

[placement, research, governance, clubs, infrastructure, about] Four innovations from the college received recognition at the IEDC Idea Fest 2023. The Research and Development Cell (RDC) achieved the granting of four patents and the publication of three patents. Additionally, the RDC contributed 30 conference papers and 12 publications. These efforts reflect the institution’s commitment to quality, innovation, and research, fostering a robust environment for academic and entrepreneurial growth. The IQAC ensures continuous improvement through structured planning and monitoring processes. Quality Policy Quality Policy  data statement blah blah balahhhh blah blah balahhhh blah blah balahhhh

[faculty, research] Strategies Overview add that top text here Key Strategies Ensuring timely, efficient and progressive performance of academic, administrative and financial tasks The relevance and quality of academic and research programmes Equitable access to and affordability of academic programmes for various sections of society Optimization and integration of modern methods of teaching and learning The credibility of evaluation procedures Ensuring the adequacy, maintenance and functioning of the support structure and services Research sharing and networking with other institutions in India and abroad

[research, infrastructure, about] Vision & Mission Vision To make quality the defining element of higher education in India through a combination of self and external quality evaluation, promotion and sustenance initiatives. Mission To arrange for periodic assessment and accreditation of institutions of higher education or units thereof, or specific academic programmes or projects; To stimulate the academic environment for promotion of quality of teaching-learning and research in higher education institutions To encourage self-evaluation, accountability, autonomy and innovations in higher education To undertake quality-related research studies, consultancy and training programmes, and To collaborate with other stakeholders of higher education for quality evaluation, promotion and sustenance. [examination, research, clubs, infrastructure, about] About NDLI Club Learn about NDLI Club and its mission NDLI CLUB National Digital Library of India (NDLI) is a virtual repository of learning resources which is not just a repository with search/browse facilities but provides a host of services for the learner community. It is sponsored and mentored by Ministry of Education, Government of India, through its National Mission on Education through Information and Communication Technology (NMEICT). Filtered and federated searching is employed to facilitate focused searching so that learners can find the right resource with least effort and in minimum time.

[examination, research, clubs, infrastructure, about] Filtered and federated searching is employed to facilitate focused searching so that learners can find the right resource with least effort and in minimum time. NDLI provides user group-specific services such as Examination Preparatory for School and College students and job aspirants. Services for Researchers and general learners are also provided. NDLI is designed to hold content of any language and provides interface support for 10 most widely used Indian languages. It is built to provide support for all academic levels including researchers and life-long learners, all disciplines, all popular forms of access devices and differently-abled learners.

[faculty, admissions, department_cse, department_ece, placement, governance, clubs, infrastructure, mou] collaboration for curriculum enhancement We have constituted a Department Advisory Committee (DAC) for the Department of Computer Science to plan the activities for the upcoming semester and to review the department’s performance in the previous semester. The primary objective of the committee is to identify curricular gaps in the Computer Science program and to propose suitable add-on courses, value-added programs, and other academic initiatives to bridge these gaps. The committee consists of distinguished Alumni, Academicians, Industry Experts from the IT sector, PTWA members, the Head of the Department, the Program Coordinator, and Senior Faculty Members.

[faculty, admissions, examination, department_cse, department_ece, placement, research, governance, clubs, infrastructure, about, mou] Studies (BoS) of the Department of Computer Science and Engineering is a statutory academic body responsible for designing, reviewing, and continuously updating the curriculum to ensure academic excellence and industry relevance. Comprising senior academicians, industry professionals, and researchers, the BoS ensures that the syllabus reflects current technological advancements, research trends, and professional requirements. It plays a vital role in maintaining academic standards, integrating Outcome-Based Education (OBE) principles, and aligning programs with regulatory guidelines while preparing students to meet global career and higher education opportunities.

[faculty, admissions, department_cse, department_ece, placement, research, clubs, infrastructure, mou] the future Dr. Ambily Francis Head of Department Electronics and Communication Engineering Qualifications Ph.D. Electronics and Communication Engineering 15+ Years Experience Welcome to Electronics and Communication Engineering The Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering at Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology is one of the pioneering departments of the institution, established in 2002. Over the years, the department has grown into a centre of academic excellence, offering B.Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering, M.Tech in Embedded Systems, and Ph.D. research programmes, catering to the evolving demands of industry and research.
[search aliases: AmbilyFrancis | ambilyfrancis | Electronicsand]

[faculty, admissions, department_cse, department_ece, placement, research, clubs, infrastructure, mou] Over the years, the department has grown into a centre of academic excellence, offering B.Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering, M.Tech in Embedded Systems, and Ph.D. research programmes, catering to the evolving demands of industry and research. The department is supported by a team of highly qualified and dedicated faculty members who actively engage in teaching, research, consultancy, and professional development. Our academic environment is strengthened by well-equipped laboratories and advanced research facilities that provide students with hands-on exposure to emerging technologies. Notable among these are the C2S VLSI Laboratory granted under MeitY, Cyber-

[faculty, department_ece, placement, research, announcement, infrastructure, mou] Physical Systems (CPS) Laboratory in collaboration with IICT, Germany, and an IoT Laboratory funded by IIT Bombay. These specialized labs enable students to gain practical experience in VLSI design, embedded systems, IoT, and cyber-physical systems. To bridge academia and industry, the department maintains active Memoranda of Understanding (MoUs) with reputed organizations including C-DAC, IIT Palakkad, Star Innovations, and Inker Robotics, along with several other industry and research partners. These collaborations facilitate joint research, training programs, internships, and student projects.

[faculty, department_ece, placement, research, announcement, infrastructure, mou] These collaborations facilitate joint research, training programs, internships, and student projects. Our students have consistently secured prestigious internships and project placements at leading institutions and organizations such as IIT Palakkad, CUSAT, DRDO, NIT Surathkal, C-DAC, Hycon, WACE Electronics, and Inker Robotics. Such opportunities provide valuable exposure to real-world engineering challenges and research culture, enhancing their professional competence. Innovation and research culture are strongly encouraged in the department.

[faculty, department_ece, placement, research, announcement, infrastructure, mou] Innovation and research culture are strongly encouraged in the department. Students actively participate in national-level project competitions, hackathons, and technical events, winning awards and project grants regularly. Industrial training is an integral part of the curriculum, ensuring that graduates are industry-ready and globally competitive. With strong academic foundations, advanced infrastructure, industry collaborations, and a focus on research and innovation, the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering continues to nurture competent engineers and researchers who contribute meaningfully to technological advancement and nation-building.

The BoS ensures that the program outcomes, teaching–learning processes, and assessment methods maintain high standards of quality to prepare graduates for successful careers in electronics, communication systems, embedded technologies, research, and entrepreneurship.

The committee ensures that teaching–learning processes, assessment methods, and program outcomes meet high academic and professional standards. It focuses on strengthening core engineering competencies, promoting innovation and research, integrating industry-relevant skills, and preparing students for careers in power systems, electronics, renewable energy, automation, embedded systems, and other evolving domains of electrical engineering.

[faculty, admissions, department_cse, department_ece, department_eee, placement, research, announcement, clubs, infrastructure, about] The curtains of AESON was raised by Mr. Muralidharan (Deputy Chief Engineer, KSEB) on 17th September 2012. The AESON of Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology represents students within the Electrical and Electronics Engineering Department. Our goal is to organize social and professional activities which strengthen and enrich the community within the department, and to help publicize various research activities within the department. We organize social events, host talks by guest speakers and students, and help communicate with the department and the students.

[faculty, admissions, department_cse, department_ece, department_eee, placement, research, announcement, clubs, infrastructure, about] We organize social events, host talks by guest speakers and students, and help communicate with the department and the students. Vision “To create an environment where creativity, research, and technological advancement thrive, enabling students to become pioneers in electrical and related engineering fields.” Mission “To build a collaborative community that nurtures creativity, practical competence, and problem-solving abilities, while providing platforms for hands-on learning, research, and career development.” Association Objectives Knowledge Advancement Through EEE Programs To further the knowledge and awareness in the field of Electrical and E

[faculty, admissions, department_ece, department_bme, placement, research, announcement, clubs, infrastructure, about, mou] (EBSA) is the official student–faculty body of the Department of Biomedical Engineering. It serves as a collaborative platform that bridges students, faculty, and industry professionals to promote academic excellence, technical competence, and professional growth. EBSA organizes technical events, workshops, seminars, community outreach, and innovation challenges to nurture creativity and leadership among Biomedical Engineering students. Vision To empower students to become innovative, ethical, and socially responsible biomedical engineers capable of transforming healthcare through technology and research.

[faculty, admissions, department_ece, department_bt, placement, research, governance, clubs, infrastructure, about, mou] We offer a B.Tech program with an intake of 60 students each year. Initially following the KTU curriculum, the department transitioned to an autonomous system in 2024, giving us more flexibility to improve and update our academic programs. In addition to the undergraduate course, we also offer an M.Tech program in Industrial Biotechnology, which admits six students annually. The department is also offers  Ph.D. programs in biotechnology. Our faculty members are dedicated to teaching, research, and student support. We focus on giving students a strong foundation in both theory and practical skills, preparing them for careers in research, industry, or further studies.

PO1 Engineering Knowledge: Apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems PO2 Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering sciences.

[faculty, admissions, department_cse, department_ece, department_bt, placement, research, governance, clubs, infrastructure] PO1 Engineering Knowledge: Apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems PO2 Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering sciences. PO3 Design/ Development of Solutions: Design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet the specified needs with appropriate consideration for the public health and safety, and the cultural, societal, and environmental considerations.

[faculty, admissions, examination, department_cse, department_ece, department_eee, department_bt, department_bme, placement, research, governance, clubs, infrastructure, about] Outcomes On successful completion of the programme, graduates will demonstrate the following 3 program specific outcomes.

[faculty, admissions, examination, department_cse, department_ece, department_eee, department_bt, department_bme, placement, research, governance, clubs, infrastructure, about] Outcomes On successful completion of the programme, graduates will demonstrate the following 3 program specific outcomes. PSO 1 To measure, model and manipulate properties of biological systems at the cellular and molecular level so as to produce sustainable products that address environmental and ethical standards PSO 2 To formulate and execute quantitative and design- oriented analysis of biological systems with modern tools and techniques that generate new knowledge at the interface of engineering and biology PSO 3 To transform as socially relevant biological engineers having a professional outlook that would enable them to work as a part of a team in an industrial, research or entrepreneurial set up and sustain a desire for higher learning and research.

[faculty, admissions, department_cse, department_ece, department_eee, placement, research, announcement, infrastructure] 1 Patents 4 Books Contact Information vishnurajan@sahrdaya.ac.in Staff Room, Bio Block Vaishnav, Edanad.p.o, Chengannur Areas of Interest No areas specified Memberships Ieee Biography Dr. Vishnu Rajan is an Associate Professor in the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering at Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology, Kerala, where he is dedicated to advancing teaching, technology, and innovation. He holds a PhD in Signal Processing and has strong interests in mobile technologies, embedded systems, and the applications of AI/ML in practical domains.

[faculty, admissions, department_cse, department_ece, department_eee, placement, research, announcement, infrastructure] He holds a PhD in Signal Processing and has strong interests in mobile technologies, embedded systems, and the applications of AI/ML in practical domains. In addition to his academic role at Sahrdaya, he is the Founder of BitSky, an MSME recognized by the Government of India under Udyam. Through BitSky, he develops and publishes Android applications on the Google Play Store, with more than 15 published apps, including 11 under the BitSky brand. His expertise lies in Kotlin and Jetpack Compose, and he continues to innovate in Android development.

[faculty, admissions, department_cse, department_ece, department_eee, placement, research, announcement, infrastructure] His expertise lies in Kotlin and Jetpack Compose, and he continues to innovate in Android development. Dr. Rajan also contributes nationally as a Subject Matter Expert in Android App Development at Internshala and as a Google Certified Educator, delivering training programs and sessions across India. He has played a central role in organizing impactful events such as the Great Indian Hackathon (2024 & 2025), Google AI Campus Fest 2023, Google I/O Extended Thrissur 2022, and Android Campus Fest Thrissur 2022, which brought together industry leaders, Google Developer Experts, and students.

[faculty, department_ece, research, announcement, about] At Sahrdaya, he has been instrumental in hosting Faculty Development Programs (FDPs), symposiums, and workshops supported by AICTE, KTU, and KSCSTE. His efforts reflect a strong commitment to building a vibrant academic and developer community, while also strengthening Sahrdaya’s position as a hub for innovation and technology. In his spare time, Dr. Rajan enjoys working on Android development with Jetpack Compose, staying curious about emerging technologies and their potential to shape the future.

[faculty, admissions, department_ece, department_eee, department_ce, placement, research, infrastructure, about] She currently serves as the Associate Professor and Head of the Department of Civil Engineering at Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology, Thrissur. She earned her Ph.D. in Civil Engineering (Transportation Engineering) from the National Institute of Technology Calicut in 2021, following a Master’s degree in Urban Engineering from Anna University, Chennai, and a Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering from Calicut University. Her academic career includes teaching and research positions at renowned institutions such as the National Institute of Technology Calicut, Cambridge Institute of Technology Bangalore, and MVJ College of Engineering, Bangalore.
[search aliases: andHead]

[faculty, admissions, department_ece, department_eee, department_ce, placement, research, infrastructure, about] Her academic career includes teaching and research positions at renowned institutions such as the National Institute of Technology Calicut, Cambridge Institute of Technology Bangalore, and MVJ College of Engineering, Bangalore. She has also contributed to research projects in public transport optimization at Anna University, Chennai. Dr. Drisya has published widely in reputed journals and conferences, including Travel Behaviour and Society (Elsevier), European Transport, and the Transportation Research Board (TRB) Annual Meetings in Washington, D.C. Her research primarily focuses on urban traffic flow, level of service analysis, and simulation-based studies for congestion management.

[faculty, admissions, department_ece, department_eee, department_ce, placement, research, infrastructure, about] Her research primarily focuses on urban traffic flow, level of service analysis, and simulation-based studies for congestion management. She has received notable recognitions, including the Best Paper Award at the 7th Conference

[faculty, department_eee, department_ce, research, announcement, governance, infrastructure, about, mou] on Transportation Systems Engineering and Management (2020) and the Best Teacher Award (2013). She has also contributed to departmental growth, leading her team in achieving NBA Accreditation for the Civil Engineering program at Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology in 2022 and 2025. Beyond academics, she actively engages in professional service as a reviewer for international journals and conferences, and holds professional memberships in ISTE and IEEE. She has also been a key organizer of workshops, training programs, and faculty development initiatives in emerging areas of Civil Engineering, GIS, Road Safety, and Sustainable Infrastructure.

[faculty, admissions, department_cse, department_ece, department_eee, department_bt, placement, research, announcement] He completed his primary education at Central Upper Primary School, Nattika, and his secondary education at Government High School Valappad, Thrissur, both in Malayalam medium.
